items.summary: Summary of minutes of the meetings for the Ministerial Group for Digital Inclusion.

items.title: Research: Retained EU law and assimilated law dashboard
items.summary: This dashboard shows a list of retained EU law (REUL) and assimilated law. These are laws that the UK saved to ensure legislative continuity immediately after Brexit.

items.summary: Framework document between the National Energy System Operator (NESO) and the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ).

items.summary: Summary of the business case for Post Office strategic transformation plan (STP) programme business case (PBC).

items.title: Make Work Pay: ending one-sided flexibility – reforms of zero hours and similar contracts
items.summary: We are seeking views on reforms relating to zero hours and similar contracts, to implement measures in the Employment Rights Act 2025 to end one-sided flexibility.

items.title: Policy paper: Unpaid carers action plan: recognise, refer, reach
items.summary: This action plan aims to improve the lives of unpaid carers in England.

items.summary: Letters to Lost Chances about the redress scheme for family members of postmasters affected by the Horizon scandal.

items.summary: Details of how we collect, handle and share the personal data of individuals who apply to register for the Horizon Family Members Redress Scheme.

items.summary: DBT paper on practical NTM estimation for computable general equilibrium (CGE) modelling using best practice gravity.

items.title: Research: Retained EU Law (REUL) and Assimilated Law Parliamentary Reports
items.summary: The reports fulfil a statutory duty to provide updates as set out in the REUL Act.

items.title: UK launches Call for Input on deepening trade relationships
items.summary: UK launches Call for Input on trade relationships with Indonesia, the Philippines, the United Arab Emirates and Uruguay, as CPTPP accession discussions advance

items.title: Form: Young Voices for Climate and Nature
items.summary: We're asking young people from across the UK to share their ideas about climate and nature action.

items.title: Form: Youth Climate and Nature Panel
items.summary: We're creating a new Youth Climate and Nature Panel to put the voices of younger people at the heart of climate and nature policies. Find out how you can take part.

items.title: Notice: Youth Climate and Nature Panel: privacy notice
items.summary: This notice sets out how we will use your personal data, and your rights when applying for the Youth Climate and Nature Panel.

items.title: Notice: Young Voices for Climate and Nature: privacy notice
items.summary: This notice sets out how we will use your personal data when submitting an entry to Young Voices for Climate and Nature.

items.summary: We are seeking input on how the UK can deepen trade relationships with Indonesia, the Philippines, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and Uruguay.

items.summary: Description of the methods and data used to produce the large businesses' payment practices and performance statistics.

items.title: Statistics at DBT
items.summary: We publish statistics on business populations, building materials, small business surveys, international trade, strategic export controls, foreign direct investment and inward investment.

items.summary: Road fuel price statistics providing average UK retail 'pump' prices on a weekly basis.

items.summary: Pre-release access list for the statistics on large businesses' payment practices and performance.

items.summary: Environmental regulations and guidance on offshore oil and gas exploration and production offshore gas unloading and storage and offshore carbon dioxide storage activities

items.title: Carbon Budgets
items.summary: A carbon budget places a restriction on the total amount of greenhouse gases the UK can emit over a 5-year period. The UK is the first country to set legally binding carbon budgets.

items.title: Policy paper: Clean flexibility roadmap
items.summary: A roadmap setting out the government’s vision for a clean, flexible, consumer-focused electricity system.

items.title: Consumer-led flexibility: consumer engagement
items.summary: We're seeking views on proposals on the best approach to engage consumers on consumer-led flexibility.

items.title: Guidance: Taking part in the UK Emissions Trading Scheme markets
items.summary: This guidance explains the operation of the UK ETS markets and how to take part in auctions and in trading on the secondary market.

items.title: Notice: Lynemouth Power Station: signature of low-carbon dispatchable Contract for Difference
items.summary: Four-year low-carbon dispatchable (LCD) CfD signed with Lynemouth Power Station, reducing subsidy and strengthening sustainability.

items.title: Official Statistics: Road fuel sales, deliveries and stock levels: 27 September 2026
items.summary: Official statistics in development on average road fuel sales, deliveries and stock levels at sampled filling stations in the United Kingdom from 27 January 2020 to 27 September 2026.

items.title: Research: The suitability of reversible air-to-air heat pumps for UK domestic use
items.summary: Detailed modelling assessing energy performance, cost, and overall suitability of reversible air‑to‑air heat pumps in a range of UK home archetypes and scenarios.

items.title: Research: Homes for Net Zero research programme
items.summary: Homes for Net Zero is a longitudinal in-home energy monitoring trial providing research insight on homeowner transitions to Net Zero.

items.summary: Regulations and guidance on fees and charging for environmental and other regulatory functions for offshore oil and gas exploration and production, offshore gas unloading and storage and offshore carbon dioxide storage activities.

items.summary: Data on the UK’s renewables sector, including capacity, electricity generation and liquid biofuels consumption.

items.summary: Official statistics in development on average road fuel sales, deliveries and stock levels
